title: The state of social media in 2026: Data from Sprout's latest pulse survey
tldr: New survey data shows brands have less control of their image online than ever, as audiences and platforms reshape brand perception. Useful benchmark for any content/comms strategy.
why_fresh: fresh 2026 survey data on a real shift in brand control
angle: Write on why the curated brand storefront is dead and what 'losing control' means for content governance.

title: The human equation: Five signals from Omnicom Health's NEXT: Innovation Week 2026
tldr: Five takeaways on human-centered communication in an AI-saturated industry, framing emotion and humanity as differentiators. Directly aligned with people-over-polish content.
why_fresh: human-centered content trend signal from an industry event
angle: Argue that 'humanity' is becoming the only defensible content moat in an AI-flooded feed.

title: Video Podcasts Show Their Marketing Power Among Gen Z In New Pizza Ad Study
tldr: A new ad study finds video podcasts outperform other formats with Gen Z. Concrete data on shifting media-consumption habits and format effectiveness.
why_fresh: new study on format-level consumption shift
angle: Break down why video podcasting beats polished ads with Gen Z — and what it means for B2B.

title: Forbes tests a creator-led audience play to grow off-platform reach
tldr: Forbes is tapping creators and their audiences to grow beyond its own platform, with a structure distinct from peers. A concrete publisher case study in the creator-publisher hybrid model.
angle: Examine how publishers borrowing creator audiences reshapes owned vs earned media strategy.

title: What Coach's &Coach reveals about Gen Z brand platforms
tldr: Breakdown of Coach's &Coach sub-brand and what it signals about building brand platforms for Gen Z. A current campaign/brand-architecture case study.
angle: Use &Coach to explore how legacy brands build separate content platforms to reach Gen Z.

title: Cannes Briefing: Cannes is selling AI transformation. Off stage the talk is about the bill
tldr: While Cannes stages tout AI transformation, the real conversation is the unspoken cost of it all. A useful reality-check on AI hype in the content/ad industry.
why_fresh: contrarian read on AI hype vs cost
angle: Write on the gap between AI keynote promises and the real bill landing on content teams.

title: Workato's First Thought Leadership Hire Connects Content Directly to Pipeline
tldr: Workato's debut thought-leadership hire ties content output straight to sales pipeline. A concrete model for proving content's commercial value.
why_fresh: new role tying thought leadership to revenue
angle: Make the case for thought leadership measured by pipeline, not impressions.

title: Your AI strategy may be training employees to stop thinking
tldr: Argues over-reliance on AI tooling risks eroding employee critical thinking — a contrarian angle on internal AI adoption and change management.
angle: Explore the cognitive cost of AI-first workflows for content and comms teams.

title: Code and Theory Teams With Adobe to Help Sports Teams Operate Like Media Companies
tldr: Sports teams are being rebuilt as media/content operations via an Adobe partnership. A clear case of the 'every brand is a publisher' thesis going operational.
angle: Use sports teams as a model for any org building an in-house content/newsroom function.

title: What silence really costs: Communicators & founders share their stories of mental health
tldr: Communicators and founders open up about mental health, modeling vulnerability and human storytelling in a polish-obsessed profession.
why_fresh: recurring awareness-month theme but human angle
angle: Reflect on humanity over polish in how communicators tell their own stories.

title: Book reviews are an endangered species. So this independent bookstore decided to start publishing its own.
tldr: As newspapers axe book sections, an indie bookstore launches its own reviews — a grassroots owned-media response to media decay.
why_fresh: novel owned-media response to content decay
angle: How brands and small orgs can fill the void left by collapsing legacy media verticals.

title: I Built a Content Engine That Creates 21 Social Posts a Week Using the Buffer API
tldr: A hands-on case study of building an automated content engine producing 21 posts weekly via API. Practical content-ops automation breakdown.
angle: Show how modular content + automation can scale output without losing voice.

title: Digital Consumer Trends 2026
tldr: Deloitte's annual read on how people use devices, media and content in 2026. Foundational media-consumption data for strategy.
angle: Pull the most counterintuitive consumption shifts and what they mean for content pillars.

title: PSP's Human-Centered Marketing Strategy Turns Emotion into Brand Strength
tldr: A case study on building brand strength through emotion-led, human-centered marketing rather than feature messaging.
angle: Decode the mechanics of turning emotion into measurable brand equity.
